helicopter__inverted_autorotation.text:
# Inverted Autorotation

## What This Maneuver Is
You roll the helicopter upside-down, then cut collective to *negative flat pitch*, letting the rotor windmill and maintain RPM through descent ГѓВѓГ‚ВѓГѓВ‚Г‚ВўГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚В” all while inverted. You recover by re-engaging negative collective and pulling out.

## Why It Is Challenging
Your brain inverts every control input. Cyclic directions reverse completely. "Pulling collective" now means pushing the stick down. RPM management works backward ГѓВѓГ‚ВѓГѓВ‚Г‚ВўГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚В” you're adding negative pitch to maintain head speed, not positive. Two mental inversions happening simultaneously under time pressure.

## Before You Start
- Solid inverted hover (30+ seconds, consistent)
- Normal autorotation entries and recoveries ГѓВѓГ‚ВѓГѓВ‚Г‚ВўГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚В” automatic, no thinking
- Inverted circuits with confidence
- Understand negative pitch range on your collective (typically -12 to -14 degrees)

## The Stick Movements

**Collective (left stick vertical):** Push DOWN to increase negative pitch ГѓВѓГ‚ВѓГѓВ‚Г‚ВўГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚В” this is your "throttle hold" equivalent inverted. Too little negative pitch = RPM bleeds. Too much = RPM overspeeds.

**Cyclic (right stick):** ALL directions are reversed. Push right, helicopter rolls left. Push forward, tail goes up (nose drops toward ground). Think "opposite everything."

**Tail rotor (left stick horizontal):** Still corrects yaw, but torque direction is reversed ГѓВѓГ‚ВѓГѓВ‚Г‚ВўГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚В” anticipate opposite pedal inputs.

## Step-by-Step Progression

**Phase 1 ГѓВѓГ‚ВѓГѓВ‚Г‚ВўГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚В” Inverted Hover to Throttle Cut**
Goal: Feel RPM decay inverted. Enter stable inverted hover at 6m, cut throttle, immediately add negative collective. Hold 3 seconds, re-engage.
*Mistake:* Freezing on collective. *Fix:* Pre-set your hand position before cutting.
*Advance when:* RPM stays in band consistently.

**Phase 2 ГѓВѓГ‚ВѓГѓВ‚Г‚ВўГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚В” Controlled Inverted Descent**
Goal: Maintain altitude loss rate of ~1m/sec. Modulate negative collective to manage RPM ГѓВѓГ‚ВѓГѓВ‚Г‚ВўГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚В” treat it like a normal auto but push instead of pull.
*Mistake:* Over-pitching, RPM spikes. *Fix:* Smaller collective inputs, feel the rotor.
*Advance when:* 10-second descents with stable RPM.

**Phase 3 ГѓВѓГ‚ВѓГѓВ‚Г‚ВўГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚В” Cyclic Control During Descent**
Goal: Maintain heading and attitude while descending. Add small cyclic corrections.
*Mistake:* Overcorrecting because brain defaults to upright logic. *Fix:* Verbalize "opposite" before each correction.
*Advance when:* Heading holds within 15 degrees.

**Phase 4 ГѓВѓГ‚ВѓГѓВ‚Г‚ВўГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚В” Entry From Forward Flight**
Goal: Inverted forward pass ГѓВѓГ‚ВѓГѓВ‚Г‚ВўГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚В†ГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚В’ roll inverted ГѓВѓГ‚ВѓГѓВ‚Г‚ВўГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚В†ГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚В’ throttle cut ГѓВѓГ‚ВѓГѓВ‚Г‚ВўГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚В†ГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚В’ autorotation.
*Mistake:* Rolling inverted too slowly, losing energy. *Fix:* Commit to the roll aggressively.
*Advance when:* Entry is consistent, no scrambling.

**Phase 5 ГѓВѓГ‚ВѓГѓВ‚Г‚ВўГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚В” Full Recovery**
Goal: Flare inverted (push cyclic forward = nose pitches toward ground/bleeds speed), then roll upright and catch with positive collective.
*Mistake:* Rolling upright too late with no RPM left. *Fix:* Begin recovery at 4m minimum.
*Advance when:* Recovery is smooth, no RPM collapse.

## Warning Signs You Need More Practice
- Consistently losing tail during descent
- RPM drops below 80% before you react
- Cyclic reversals still require conscious thought
- Recovery altitude keeps shrinking dangerously

## Sim Tips
1. Slow sim speed to 70% until Phase 3
2. Display RPM gauge prominently on screen
3. Practice inverted hover with eyes closed briefly ГѓВѓГ‚ВѓГѓВ‚Г‚ВўГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚В” builds feel over sight
4. Repeat Phase 5 recoveries 20x in one session ГѓВѓГ‚ВѓГѓВ‚Г‚ВўГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚ВЂГѓВѓГ‚В‚ГѓВ‚Г‚В” muscle memory only

helicopter__pirouette_to_inverted.text:
# Pirouette to Inverted

## What This Maneuver Is
A continuous yaw rotation (pirouette) that transitions seamlessly through upright into **fully inverted flight**, maintaining altitude and position throughout.

---

## Why It Is Challenging
Your brain must flip its entire control mapping mid-rotation. Collective that saves you upright **kills you inverted**. Aileron and elevator reverse. You must anticipate the inversion point *before* it arrives and pre-load negative collective ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” all while the helicopter is spinning.

---

## Before You Start
- Solid inverted hover (60+ seconds, no drama)
- Clean stationary pirouettes both directions
- Inverted forward/backward/sideways flight
- Basic piro-flips at reduced speed

---

## The Stick Movements

| Control | What It Does |
|--------|--------------|
| **Rudder** | Drives continuous yaw ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” keep it constant, don't stab |
| **Collective** | Transitions from positive ГѓВўГ‚В†Г‚В’ through zero ГѓВўГ‚В†Г‚В’ negative as nose crosses inverted |
| **Aileron/Elevator** | Constantly corrected as orientation changes ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” inputs *reverse* when inverted |
| **Rate** | Slow pirouette (ГѓВ‚Г‚Вј speed) initially ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” speed is the enemy while learning |

---

## Step-by-Step Progression

**Phase 1 ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” Find the Inversion Point**
Slow pirouette, stop exactly inverted. Hold 5 seconds. Repeat until stopping precisely inverted is automatic.
*Mistake:* Overshooting past inverted. *Fix:* Anticipate 15ГѓВ‚Г‚В° early.
*Advance when:* You stop inverted 8/10 attempts, no altitude loss.

**Phase 2 ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” Collective Transition Practice**
From upright hover, manually roll inverted (no pirouette). Practice smoothly swapping from positive to negative collective.
*Mistake:* Pause at zero collective ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” you'll drop. *Fix:* Treat it like a door, not a stop sign. Keep moving through it.
*Advance when:* Transition takes under 1 second, altitude stays within 3 feet.

**Phase 3 ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” Slow Pirouette Through Inverted**
ГѓВ‚Г‚Вј throttle pirouette. Cross through inverted, maintain 2 seconds, come back upright.
*Mistake:* Collective swap happens too late. *Fix:* Start going negative at **90ГѓВ‚Г‚В° before** full inverted.
*Advance when:* Clean, no altitude spike, no panic inputs.

**Phase 4 ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” Full 360ГѓВ‚Г‚В° Pirouette**
One complete rotation maintaining constant altitude throughout.
*Mistake:* Slowing the pirouette rate when inverted. *Fix:* Lock that rudder input ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” consistent speed builds consistent muscle memory.
*Advance when:* 5 clean rotations in a row.

**Phase 5 ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” Speed and Precision**
Increase pirouette rate. Add forward/backward positioning corrections while spinning.
*Mistake:* Correcting position with wrong aileron direction. *Fix:* Talk yourself through it: "inverted, inputs reversed."
*Advance when:* Looks boring to watch.

---

## Warning Signs You Need More Practice
- Altitude spikes or drops **every** time you cross inverted
- You hesitate on collective at the inversion point
- Inverted hover degrades under 30 seconds when nervous
- You're mentally "guessing" aileron direction past 180ГѓВ‚Г‚В°

---

## Sim Tips
1. **Enable slow-motion** ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” watch exactly where collective changes
2. **Map a kill switch** ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” reset fast, remove fear
3. **Practice inverted hover first thing every session** ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” keep it sharp
4. **Record replays** ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” your hands feel smooth; the replay shows the truth

helicopter__backwards_flight.text:
# Backwards Flight ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” RC Helicopter

## What This Maneuver Is
Flying the helicopter tail-first toward you in a controlled, straight path ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” cyclic, tail, and throttle working together while your orientation is completely mirrored.

## Why It Is Challenging
Your brain still maps "back cyclic = helicopter moves back." But the helicopter's nose is pointing away, so **left is right, right is left** on aileron. Elevator stays logical; aileron is fully reversed. Split-second correction instincts will kill you here.

## Before You Start
- Rock-solid nose-in hovering
- Confident pirouette corrections
- Figure-8s in all orientations
- Tail-in and side-in circuits completed cleanly

## The Stick Movements
| Control | What it does in backwards flight |
|---|---|
| **Collective** | Same ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” altitude control, no change |
| **Tail rotor** | Same direction feel ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” left stick left/right still yaws normally |
| **Back cyclic (elevator)** | Pushes helicopter **toward you** ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” this feels natural |
| **Left aileron** | Moves helicopter **right** ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” completely backwards |
| **Right aileron** | Moves helicopter **left** ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” completely backwards |

**Golden rule:** Elevator is your friend. Aileron is the enemy until rewired.

## Step-by-Step Progression

**Phase 1 ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” Static Backwards Hover**
Hover tail-in 10 feet out, 5 feet high. Hold position 60 seconds. Don't move laterally. *Mistake: Overcorrecting aileron. Fix: Smaller inputs, pause between corrections.* Advance when stable for 2 minutes.

**Phase 2 ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” Backwards Hover Lateral Shuffles**
Intentionally drift left/right 3 feet and correct back. Burn in reversed aileron reflex deliberately. *Mistake: Panic reverting to forward-flight instincts. Fix: Verbalize "opposite" aloud.* Advance when 10 corrections feel automatic.

**Phase 3 ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” Slow Backwards Tracking**
Pull back cyclic gently, fly 20 feet toward yourself, stop. Maintain heading ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” no weathervaning. *Mistake: Tail drifting sideways during translation. Fix: Lead with tiny tail rotor corrections.* Advance when 5 clean passes completed.

**Phase 4 ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” Backwards Rectangle**
Fly backward, 90ГѓВ‚Г‚В° yaw, fly backward again ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” four sides. Keep altitude constant. *Mistake: Skidding the corners. Fix: Stop forward motion before yawing.* Advance when rectangle looks square, not rhombus-shaped.

**Phase 5 ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” Backwards Figure-8**
Two backward circles joined. Aileron reversals happen constantly here. Speed controlled, altitude locked. *Mistake: Rushing the connecting point. Fix: Treat each loop separately.* Advance when completed 3 times without correction bailout.

**Phase 6 ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” Speed and Distance**
Push speed up. Double the distance. Add light wind conditions in sim. This is mission-ready backwards flight.

## Warning Signs ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” Stop and Rebuild
- Consistently correcting the wrong aileron direction
- Altitude surging during directional inputs
- Losing heading within 5 seconds of backwards translation
- Requiring full-deflection corrections to recover

## Sim Tips
1. Set a **visual marker** on screen ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” fly backward toward it, not randomly
2. Use **slow-motion replay** ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” watch your stick inputs vs helicopter response
3. Practice Phase 1 **eyes closed briefly** to feel trim, not see it
4. Map a **panic button** to instantly pause ГѓВўГ‚ВЂГ‚В” analyze before resetting

helicopter__rolling_circle.text:
# Rolling Circle ГўВЂВ” RC Helicopter

## What This Maneuver Is
Fly a perfect horizontal circle while simultaneously rolling the helicopter continuously around its longitudinal axis. The nose traces the circle; the fuselage spins like a barrel roll the entire time.

## Why It Is Challenging
Your brain must decouple two simultaneous tasks: maintaining circular flight geometry AND managing a constantly-changing roll orientation. Every 90Г‚В° of roll changes which stick input creates lift versus drift. Cyclic inputs rotate with the helicopter ГўВЂВ” what was "up elevator" is now "left aileron" halfway through the roll. You're essentially re-mapping your inputs 360Г‚В° continuously.

## Before You Start
- Solid inverted hover (30+ seconds, hands relaxed)
- Clean barrel rolls with altitude recovery
- Consistent nose-in circle at speed
- Comfortable pirouetting funnels

## The Stick Movements

**Aileron (roll):** Constant input ГўВЂВ” steady left or right roll rate throughout. This is your metronome.

**Elevator:** Rotates with the helicopter. When upright = forward push for circle. When inverted = it reverses ГўВЂВ” you push the same direction but the helicopter's reference has flipped. Think "always push toward the circle center" relative to the canopy.

**Collective:** Constantly pumping. Upright = normal positive pitch. Inverted = negative pitch to maintain altitude. Transitions happen twice per rotation at the knife-edge points.

**Rudder:** Keeps the nose tracking the circle. Minor corrections only ГўВЂВ” if you're fighting rudder, your roll rate or circle speed is wrong.

## Step-by-Step Progression

**Phase 1 ГўВЂВ” Slow Roll, Large Circle**
Goal: Establish timing. Fly a wide circle, add slow aileron roll. Focus purely on collective transitions at inverted. *Mistake: Late collective input ГўВ†В’ altitude loss. Fix: Anticipate inverted before you're there.* Advance when altitude stays within 10 feet.

**Phase 2 ГўВЂВ” Elevator Mapping**
Goal: Add active elevator to maintain circle geometry. Watch for the circle drifting outward when inverted. *Mistake: Releasing elevator when inverted. Fix: Remind yourself "push stays push."* Advance when circle shape holds through full rotations.

**Phase 3 ГўВЂВ” Tighten the Circle**
Goal: Reduce diameter by 30%. More coordinated elevator and collective needed. *Mistake: Over-correcting with rudder. Fix: Slow your roll rate instead.* Advance when 5 consecutive clean rotations.

**Phase 4 ГўВЂВ” Speed Up the Roll**
Goal: Double roll rate. Collective pumping must accelerate. *Mistake: Flat spots in the roll. Fix: Commit ГўВЂВ” no hesitation at knife-edge.* Advance when roll rate feels automatic.

**Phase 5 ГўВЂВ” Presentation Quality**
Goal: Consistent altitude, round circle, smooth roll rate. No visible corrections. Advance when judges can't see your mistakes.

## Warning Signs You Need More Practice
- Altitude drops every inverted pass
- Circle becomes an oval when inverted
- Rudder constantly sawing back and forth
- Roll rate changes mid-maneuver

## Sim Tips
- Fly directly in front of you only ГўВЂВ” eliminate perspective confusion early
- Record and replay every attempt ГўВЂВ” watch altitude trace
- Deliberately pause at inverted hover mid-practice to rebuild the inverted elevator map
- Practice slow-roll funnels first as a warm-up every session

helicopter__elevator.text:
# Elevator (RC Helicopter)

## What This Maneuver Is
A pure forward-backward pitching rotation around the helicopter's lateral axis ГўВЂВ” nose pitches up and down continuously while maintaining a fixed point in space, or flown as a pendulum arc. Think of it as looping through vertical pitch cycles without rolling or yawing.

## Why It Is Challenging
Your brain wants to add collective on the upswing and reduce it on the downswing ГўВЂВ” that's helicopter logic. Here, you must *resist* that instinct. Tail rotor authority changes dramatically as orientation shifts. Gyroscopic precession fights your inputs during rapid pitch reversals.

## Before You Start
- Solid inverted hover (10+ seconds stable)
- Comfortable nose-in pirouette corrections
- Clean tic-tocs both axes
- Can recover from unexpected inverted attitudes reflexively

## The Stick Movements

**Cyclic (Right Stick):** Drives the pitch rotation. Forward cyclic pitches nose down, back cyclic pitches nose up. You're pumping this rhythmically ГўВЂВ” the timing IS the maneuver.

**Collective (Left Stick vertical):** Must counteract altitude loss. Add collective as the disc unloads during rotation. You'll need more collective than feels natural during the upward-pitching phase.

**Tail Rotor (Left Stick horizontal):** Constant micro-corrections. Gyroscopic precession will yaw the nose as pitch rate changes ГўВЂВ” anticipate it, don't chase it.

**Throttle:** Maintain head speed. Let the governor work; don't manually compensate.

## Step-by-Step Progression

**Phase 1 ГўВЂВ” Slow Pitch Rocks**
Goal: Feel the collective demand change. Pitch 30Г‚В° nose-up, hold, return. 10 reps. *Mistake:* No collective compensation ГўВ†В’ drops altitude. *Fix:* Add 15% collective at full pitch. *Advance:* Altitude stays within 1 meter.

**Phase 2 ГўВЂВ” Full Nose-Up to Inverted Arc**
Goal: Pitch to 90Г‚В° bank then 180Г‚В° (inverted). Hold inverted 2 seconds, recover. *Mistake:* Panicking and pulling collective inverted ГўВ†В’ climbs violently. *Fix:* Reduce collective when inverted. *Advance:* Inverted hold is stable.

**Phase 3 ГўВЂВ” Continuous Slow Elevator**
Goal: Full 360Г‚В° pitch loop at slow speed, maintaining position. *Mistake:* Drifting laterally ГўВЂВ” you're adding roll without knowing it. *Fix:* Video yourself, isolate the drift axis. *Advance:* 5 consecutive loops, same spot.

**Phase 4 ГўВЂВ” Speed Up the Rotation**
Goal: Increase rotation rate to 1 loop per 3 seconds. *Mistake:* Losing tail at the top. *Fix:* Pre-load opposite rudder entering the top. *Advance:* 10 consecutive clean loops.

**Phase 5 ГўВЂВ” Precision and Combination**
Goal: Defined entry/exit points, fixed altitude, combine with lateral drift. Fly elevator while translating sideways.

## Warning Signs You Need More Practice
- Consistent altitude loss through the top of the loop
- Yaw drift exceeds 15Г‚В° per loop
- Recovery from botched inverted takes more than 1 second
- Collective inputs are reactive, not anticipatory

## Sim Tips
1. Slow motion first ГўВЂВ” dial down simulation speed to 50%
2. Enable telemetry overlays showing pitch angle numerically
3. Practice inverted hover 10 minutes before every elevator session
4. Use a grid backdrop to spot lateral drift immediately

helicopter__piro_flip.text:
# Piro Flip ГўВЂВ” Advanced RC Helicopter Training

## What This Maneuver Is
A continuous pirouette (nose spinning 360Г‚В°) combined with a flip (full cyclic roll or loop) executed simultaneously. The heli rotates on its yaw axis while tumbling through a full cyclic rotation.

---

## Why It Is Challenging
Your brain must decompose two simultaneous rotations. Cyclic inputs must **continuously chase a moving nose** ГўВЂВ” every 90Г‚В° of pirouette, your roll/pitch input rotates 90Г‚В° with it. There is no fixed forward reference. Spatial disorientation hits hard and fast.

---

## Before You Start
- Solid inverted hover (30+ seconds stable)
- Clean tail-in and nose-in flips at altitude
- Comfortable pirouette hovering in all orientations
- Pirouette while translating (basic piro circles)
- Basic piro flip attempts with rudder *stopped* mid-flip

---

## The Stick Movements

| Control | What It Does |
|---|---|
| **Left stick horizontal** | Continuous yaw ГўВЂВ” keep it pegged left or right throughout |
| **Right stick** | Executes the flip ГўВЂВ” but direction rotates with the pirouette |
| **Collective** | Pumps slightly to maintain altitude through rotation |
| **Cyclic correction** | Small constant chasing inputs to stay on-axis |

The critical concept: **if nose is 90Г‚В° to your right, your "forward" flip input is now your right stick input.**

---

## Step-by-Step Progression

**Phase 1 ГўВЂВ” Frozen Piro, Clean Flip**
Do a flip, stop rotation, check orientation, restart piro. Goal: isolate each rotation mentally.
*Mistake: rushing the next input. Fix: pause deliberately.*
Advance: 5 clean paused reps.

**Phase 2 ГўВЂВ” Slow Piro + Flip Entry**
Half-speed yaw, initiate flip at tail-in. Let piro continue through the flip.
*Mistake: killing yaw during flip. Fix: left stick stays pegged.*
Advance: flip completes without yaw stopping.

**Phase 3 ГўВЂВ” Full Speed Piro Flip, Tail-In Entry Only**
Full yaw rate, always enter from tail-in position.
*Mistake: over-correcting cyclic mid-flip. Fix: commit to the flip input, correct after.*
Advance: 5 consecutive completions landing upright.

**Phase 4 ГўВЂВ” Variable Entry Points**
Enter the flip from nose-in, side-on. Builds orientation remapping.
*Mistake: freezing on inputs. Fix: visualize the heli's nose before each attempt.*
Advance: clean entries from all four headings.

**Phase 5 ГўВЂВ” Multiple Consecutive Piro Flips**
Two or three flips in one continuous pirouette. Competition-level execution.
*Mistake: losing altitude through sequence. Fix: aggressive collective pumps between flips.*
Advance: three consecutive with controlled exit.

---

## Warning Signs You Need More Practice
- Yaw rate changes during the flip (hands fighting themselves)
- Cannot predict exit orientation before it happens
- Consistently crashes at the 180Г‚В° inverted point
- Disorientation lasts more than 1 second after completing

---

## Sim Tips
1. **Slow motion mode** ГўВЂВ” run at 50% sim speed to read your own inputs
2. **Freeze-frame crashes** ГўВЂВ” rewind and identify exactly where input broke down
3. **Overlay telemetry** ГўВЂВ” watch cyclic position during the maneuver
4. **Practice tail-in flips with eyes closed briefly** ГўВЂВ” forces proprioceptive muscle memory over visual dependency

helicopter__tail_in_circle.text:
# Tail-In Circle

## What This Maneuver Is
Helicopter flies a horizontal circle with the tail always pointing toward you. The nose traces the circle's edge while you stand at the center.

## Why It Is Challenging
Your cyclic inputs constantly rotate relative to your body. At 9 o'clock, right cyclic moves the helicopter *away* from you. At 3 o'clock, right cyclic pulls it *toward* you. Your brain must continuously remap stick direction to real-world movement while simultaneously managing rudder, collective, and throttle.

## Before You Start
- Solid nose-in hover (stationary, 60+ seconds clean)
- Confident side-in hover both directions
- Basic tail-in forward flight circuit
- Smooth rudder control with no pendulum oscillation

## The Stick Movements

| Control | Job During Circle |
|---|---|
| **Right cyclic** | Drives the circle ГўВЂВ” pushes nose along the arc |
| **Left cyclic** | Slows/stops lateral drift, tightens radius |
| **Rudder** | Continuously rotates nose to stay on the circle's tangent |
| **Forward cyclic** | Compensates nose-low attitude from translational lift |
| **Collective** | Maintains constant altitude through speed changes |

## Step-by-Step Progression

**Phase 1 ГўВЂВ” Slow Pivot Walk**
Goal: Feel rudder-cyclic coordination. Hover at 10 meters, apply minimal right cyclic, add right rudder to keep tail pointed at you. Quarter-circle only. *Mistake: Leading with rudder before cyclic ГўВЂВ” helicopter slides sideways. Fix: Cyclic moves first, rudder follows immediately.*
Advance when: Quarter-circle feels linked, not choppy.

**Phase 2 ГўВЂВ” Half Circle**
Goal: Survive the side-in transition. Complete 180Г‚В°, stop, return. *Mistake: Freezing inputs at side-in. Fix: Trust your muscle memory ГўВЂВ” keep the same rhythm.*
Advance when: Both side-in transitions feel automatic.

**Phase 3 ГўВЂВ” Full Circle, Large Radius (15+ meters)**
Goal: Complete 360Г‚В° without stopping. Large radius = more time to think. *Mistake: Varying speed, making corrections jerky. Fix: Commit to steady cyclic pressure, fix with rudder not cyclic.*
Advance when: Three consecutive clean circles each direction.

**Phase 4 ГўВЂВ” Tighten the Radius**
Goal: 8ГўВЂВ“10 meter radius. Inputs must come faster. *Mistake: Collective drops in tighter turns ГўВЂВ” translational lift decreases. Fix: Pre-add collective before tightening.*
Advance when: Tight circles match quality of large ones.

**Phase 5 ГўВЂВ” Altitude and Speed Variations**
Goal: Circle while climbing, descending, accelerating. Exposes any input dependency you've hidden. *Mistake: Altitude creep on back side of circle. Fix: Back cyclic causes climb ГўВЂВ” reduce collective proactively.*
Advance when: Smooth control through all variations.

## Warning Signs You Need More Practice
- Nose consistently drifts outward past 90Г‚В°/270Г‚В°
- Altitude changes more than 1 meter per circle
- You pause inputs mentally at side-in
- Recovery requires full stick deflection

## Sim Tips
1. **Record and replay** ГўВЂВ” watch your flight line from above to see egg shapes vs true circles
2. **Slow motion mode** ГўВЂВ” most sims have it; use it at the 9 and 3 o'clock positions specifically
3. **Place a reference marker** at your feet in the sim to reinforce standing-center discipline
4. **Practice direction changes** ГўВЂВ” immediately reverse after each circle, no repositioning pause

helicopter__hurricane.text:
# Hurricane (Horizontal Pirouette)

## What This Maneuver Is
The helicopter orbits a fixed point while continuously pirouetting ГўВЂВ” nose spinning at a constant rate while the fuselage travels in a horizontal circle around you. It looks like a spinning top orbiting a center point.

## Why It Is Challenging
Your brain tracks two simultaneous rotations. Every degree of pirouette changes which stick direction equals "out from center." You must feed continuous, dynamic cyclic corrections that rotate opposite to the pirouette ГўВЂВ” essentially counter-rotating your inputs mentally while your hands move smoothly. Most pilots can pirouette OR orbit. Combining both overwhelms working memory.

## Before You Start
- Clean pirouettes (both directions, 10+ rotations) ГўВњВ“
- Nose-in and side-in stationary hover ГўВњВ“
- Pirouette circles (orbiting without continuous piro) ГўВњВ“
- Solid idle-up/3D flight mode familiarity ГўВњВ“

## The Stick Movements
- **Rudder:** Constant deflection, maintains steady pirouette rate ГўВЂВ” set it and forget it
- **Cyclic (right stick):** This is everything. As the helicopter spins, you rotate your cyclic input continuously around the stick's center to maintain orbit ГўВЂВ” a slow circular wrist motion, always pushing "away from center" relative to the current nose heading
- **Collective:** Minor adjustments only ГўВЂВ” slight pulls if the orbit decays inward, slight pushes to prevent climb during aggressive cyclic inputs
- **Throttle/Governor:** Stable ГўВЂВ” let the governor work

## Step-by-Step Progression

**Phase 1 ГўВЂВ” Slow Piro, No Orbit**
Goal: Feel the input rotation concept. Hold a pirouette and practice moving cyclic in a slow circle without actually orbiting. Just observe how inputs must rotate. *Mistake:* Fixed cyclic direction ГўВЂВ” helicopter drifts instantly. *Fix:* Think of your right stick tracing a lazy clock face. *Advance when:* Input feels natural without drift.

**Phase 2 ГўВЂВ” Controlled Drift Into Orbit**
Goal: Initiate one slow orbit. Add cyclic rotation gradually until the helicopter starts traveling in a circle. Large radius, slow pirouette. *Mistake:* Too much cyclic too fast ГўВЂВ” orbit becomes erratic. *Fix:* Small radius inputs, patient inputs. *Advance when:* Three consistent orbits completed.

**Phase 3 ГўВЂВ” Tighten the Radius**
Goal: Reduce orbit diameter to 10-15 feet. Increase pirouette speed slightly. *Mistake:* Losing altitude on opposite side of orbit. *Fix:* Add slight collective on the back half of orbit. *Advance when:* Radius is consistent for 5 orbits.

**Phase 4 ГўВЂВ” Speed Up the Pirouette**
Goal: Match pirouette rate to a crisp 3D pace. Cyclic input rotation must accelerate to match. *Mistake:* Cyclic rotation lags behind pirouette ГўВЂВ” orbit collapses. *Fix:* Slightly anticipate the input, stay ahead of the nose. *Advance when:* Rate looks locked and intentional.

**Phase 5 ГўВЂВ” Direction Reversal**
Goal: Complete Hurricanes in both directions. *Mistake:* Dominant hand bias makes one direction sloppy. *Fix:* Log equal sim time both ways. *Advance when:* Both directions are clean for 8+ orbits.

## Warning Signs You Need More Practice
- Orbit diameter constantly changing
- Altitude drifting more than 3 feet
- Recovery requires stopping the pirouette
- Can only perform one direction cleanly

## Sim Tips
1. Enable orbit tracking overlays if available ГўВЂВ” watch your actual path vs intended circle
2. Practice at 50% speed first, then real-time
3. Use a fixed ground reference point and physically point at it between sessions to train spatial focus
4. Crash aggressively ГўВЂВ” push past comfort zone every session, then dial back for clean reps
